best_overall_lp.note: Marginal LP lead over DELTA (0.5005 vs 0.4992), within noise

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA-Matched improves from 2p to 3p while other models degrade
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: MRR 0.733->0.738 (2p->3p), only model with positive delta among 7 tested

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-hop advantage is structural, not a hyperparameter accident
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: DELTA leads GraphGPS on 3p at all 5 DropEdge rates (0-40%)

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA's compositional advantage grows with reasoning depth
Multi-seed validation confirms DELTA advantage on 3p is statistically robust
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: 3-seed: DELTA 0.742+/-0.009 vs GraphGPS 0.713+/-0.007. Std bars don't overlap

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA inference cost is comparable to GraphGPS despite 34x training cost
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: Encoding 51.8x slower but per-query scoring 0.8-0.9x GraphGPS

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable temperature keeps sharp attention (all heads retain temp > 2.0)
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: 16/16 heads (delta_matched) and 24/24 heads (delta_full) maintain temp > 2.0 at convergence

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature sharpening activates DELTA-Full's excess capacity on 3p
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: DELTA-Full temp=4.0: 3p MRR 0.4018 vs temp=1.0: 0.3725, +0.029. Dead heads 83%->38%.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Edge temps drift UP, node temps drift DOWN — edge attention benefits from sharpness, node attention prefers averaging
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: delta_full temp=4.0: L2_edge 4.029->4.524, L2_node 3.992->3.587. Consistent pattern across all layers.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Layer-specific temperature (L0 soft, L1+L2 sharp) achieves best LP MRR while reducing dead heads
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: B_layer_sharp: LP MRR=0.4783 (best of 4 conditions), dead=9/24 (38%), 3p MRR=0.3908 (+0.018 vs A)

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Node attention requires temperature sharpening to activate — edge-only sharpening insufficient
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: C_edge_sharp: L1 node heads 100% dead, L2 node 50-100% dead despite edge temps at 4.0. B with node temp=4.0: L1+L2 node heads 0% dead.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Generalization gap between val and test MRR indicates overfitting
failed_hypotheses.evidence: Weight decay sweep showed no pattern -- gap is val-set noise
Marginal effect for both models. Reframed as robustness check.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature reduces dead heads below 50% for delta_matched
failed_hypotheses.evidence: 69%->50%, missed <50% threshold by 1 head. PARTIAL for matched, CONFIRMED for full (83%->38%)

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Layer-specific or edge-only sharpening matches D's 3p improvement
failed_hypotheses.evidence: B: 3p=0.3908 (vs D 0.4018, gap=0.011). C: 3p=0.3678 (worse than A). Neither matched D's full 3p benefit.

discovered_patterns: delta_matched improves with reasoning depth while all baselines degrade monotonically
smaller DELTA (157K) beats larger DELTA (293K) on compositional reasoning -- capacity paradox
advantage gap doubles at each hop depth: +0.004, +0.026, +0.066, +0.100
DELTA encoding is 51.8x slower but per-query inference is 0.8-0.9x faster than GraphGPS
training cost is dominated by 2-hop edge adjacency computation (34x slower per epoch)
both models peak at ep 125-150, overtrain after -- early stopping critical
DELTA-Matched consistently peaks at ep 125 across all seeds and experiments
GraphGPS benefits more from DropEdge in absolute terms but can't close architectural gap
DELTA attention is mathematically degenerate: d_head=12 + degree~40 -> normalized entropy 0.87 (near-uniform). Model bypasses attention via residual connections.
cross-depth cosine similarity = 1.0 everywhere — encoding is query-independent. Compositional advantage lives in representation structure, not depth-dependent routing.
temperature sharpening helps DELTA-Full 3p (+0.029) but hurts DELTA-Matched 3p (-0.017) — smaller model's residual bypass was already optimal
Layer 0 always 100% dead regardless of temperature — first layer does neighborhood averaging, not selective attention
edge attention benefits from sharpness (temps drift UP), node attention prefers soft averaging (temps drift DOWN) — learned automatically via temperature gradient
DELTA-Full Layer 2 activates with temp=4.0 (0% dead from epoch 100 onward), explaining 3p improvement — 3rd layer unlocked by sharp attention
layer-specific temperature (B: L0=1,L1+L2=4) achieves best LP MRR of all conditions (0.4783) — selective sharpening better than uniform
node attention NEEDS explicit sharpening to activate: C (edge-only sharp) keeps L1 node 100% dead, but B (node+edge sharp at L1+L2) activates all node heads
in B, node temps at L1+L2 drift DOWN from 4.0→3.5-3.7 — optimal node temp likely between 2-3, not 4.0
condition C shows edge temps drift to 4.5 even starting from 4.0 (L0 edge → 4.46, L2 edge → 4.49) — edge attention consistently wants more sharpness
open_questions: What is the optimal node temperature? B's drift (4.0→3.5) suggests ~2-3 may be better than 4.0 (Phase 48)
Would higher edge init temp (e.g. 6.0) improve results given consistent upward drift?
Can DELTA-Full with targeted temperature match DELTA-Matched's compositional advantage?
Can DELTA-Full be compressed via importance pruning to match DELTA-Matched on 3p/5p?
Does curriculum compression during training converge to the same optimal capacity?
Why does DELTA peak at ep 125 while GraphGPS peaks at 150-200?
Would multi-seed 4p/5p confirm the accelerating advantage from Phase 44?

43.title: DropEdge robustness
43.result: CONFIRMED -- advantage structural at all 5 rates

45.title: Inference timing + multi-seed
45.result: CONFIRMED -- multi-seed robust, inference cost comparable

46.title: Attention sharpening via learnable temperature
46.result: PARTIAL -- dead heads reduced (full 83%->38%), edge/node temp divergence discovered, full 3p +0.029 but matched 3p -0.017

47.title: Layer-specific temperature initialization
47.result: PARTIAL -- B (L0 soft, L1+L2 sharp) achieves best LP MRR (0.4783) but 3p (0.3908) short of D (0.4018). Node attention requires sharpening to activate. Optimal node temp likely 2-3.
